title: Initialise Supabase JS client (shared module)
description: Create a single shared JavaScript module that initialises the Supabase client using environment variables. All auth pages import from this module.
acceptance_criteria: A file supabase.js (or supabase-client.js) exists and exports a configured Supabase client
SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are read from environment or a config constant — never hardcoded in page scripts
Importing the module and calling supabase.auth.getSession() returns a valid response object

steps.description: Test the import in browser console
steps.details: Open a page that imports supabase-client.js. In DevTools console run: const { data } = await supabase.auth.getSession(); console.log(data); — expect { session: null } when not logged in.

technical_notes: Using the ESM CDN import avoids a build step and is consistent with the current vanilla JS codebase. If a bundler is introduced later, swap the import path to the npm package.
